title: Премьера клипа: UMA2RMAN - Поговори со мной

blocks.text: I recorded this interview one day before the start of Paris photo.
I had many thoughts. I have accumulated the doubts of all the artists, photographers and creators that I feel and hear.
Pierre works with the archives of the entire Magnum agency. He is an elinsyn man who has access to such wealth.

This opportunity to look inside of all the drafts is priceless. And I like his meaningful suggestions. I think it is important to understand that work and creativity are different processes. To feel and understand what should be the core is paramount. Waiting for approval is a failure.

A series of photographs revealing the character of 5 heroes.Through the display of additional (small) photos of the work, the person represents WIDE.The viewer is given the opportunity to feel how he relates to the body. Where is its border, is it there? To feel the barrier (or lack thereof) to the photographer who takes it off is present in the moment nearby.

The issue of publicity of the body, attitude to the body in society and in social networks today is felt more and more sharply. The need for social networks to cover breasts and genitals (and priests too) causes different feelings and confusion as well. Was it worth sharing? And now, it would seem, now in an age of excessive openness, we are becoming even more closed. At the same time, as a photographer, I get a huge request for a nude photo. A man today is ready to capture that which is invisible and hidden, which has always been basic. Or not? And if for some it is still tantamount to "shocking" to show their nude photos, then for someone it is "normal", easy. The appearance of Internet channels available in every phone began to lead to the fact that borders are erased (or not), exposing all internal feelings. And not only. Through the Stroke exhibition, I wanted to understand this phenomenon, explore it and see my borders and those of others. Do we think when we see nude, what our emancipation is talking about, why more and more people want to capture themselves in pictures without clothes, will they hide these large photographs on the table and remember in old age “what it was like”, what motivation is there posturing or vice versa naturalness ..

In connection with a certain degree of closeness, I decided to show part of the frank work online, while the rest can be found offline. And so it happened with the first series of nude photos that became part of the STROKKE exhibition in Riga (2018).

“Stroke”, the first solo show by the Russian born artist Dasha Glukhova, in the Baltics. The exhibition is built around five female sitters, whose characters become apparent in the diverse ways that they approach nudity. By revealing their bodies they exhibit their inner nature and also their very different relationship to the viewer.

Mogens Andersen  X  Glukhova Dasha
Review on the joint exhibition of the Danish artist Mogens Andersen (malerandersen.dk) and the Russian photographer Daria Glukhova

glukhova-dasha.com 

Using different visual forms and techniques, Andersen and Glukhova reflect their own unique perception of the same things. At the same time the project shows photography and painting in a similar and different forms. Youth and maturity in the art vision. Five topics/titles. Five months of working separately from each other. One goal to find answers, connections or divergences of the picture. All answers you can find at the exhibition.

Ph.D. OLGA AVERYANOVA,
Head of Art of Photography department
The Pushkin Museum of Fine Arts

* These are my sister and my nephew. We went to Croatia on holiday together. I love them very much. It's a pity I can't photohraph them that often since I've had my own child. It is very difficult to show beauty in everyday life.

A photographer investigates the behaviours and places of the members of the family of different ages in the 'family landscape' as well as contradictions emerging in the life of modern people focused on family and bringing up kids and with stiff demands from life, work and economy. Kids environment becomes a shelter, which is probably appreciated more by adults. A family is a warm universe reminding adults about circulation of time, its cold and indifferent flow.

People and animals
I like the face of this model and her hair. And the Dalmatian dog, they have similar expressions on their faces and half-open mouths. And the spots should jump on the dress…

The underlying dualism, the opposition of the objective world of nature and the world of human culture, where a human, ‘a subjective spirit’ exists and he does not participate in the natural course of the world like animals do, but he separates himself from it and puts himself against it. And somewhere in the middle of this dualism lies the ‘idea of culture’. However, “play is older than culture, for culture, however inadequately defined, always presupposes human society, and animals have not waited for man to teach them their playing.”
J. Huizinga

Human and culture
I like this photo very much. I can't remember what we were talking about then, however it turned out to be exactly how I wanted it to be – sincere and genuine, and super easy.

Culture quenches a secret grief of a person limited by destiny or decision, grief for the opportunities that turned out to be unavailable. It opens them up in the images of fantasy, performances, play…, extending his or her self-awareness and broadening the horizon of life experience. Long before a human accumulated powers enough to change the world around him, he had created the 'environment' in the sphere of play-art where the opposition of play and seriousness did not matter. «We play and know that we play, so we must be more than merely rational beings, for play is irrational.» . Huizinga
I was shooting Tanya for the personal coach project. She is very feminine despite her being rigid and 'inflexible'. I think she is a wonderful coach.

Spectacular part of acrobatics has always been related to an all-time human aspiration to physical perfection as well as archaic cruelty and subconscious desire of spectators to witness a tragedy, a failure to perform a trick. An acrobat is a Greek word which stands for a person who walks on tiptoe carefully climbing up. The playful perversion here is hypocrisy, disengagement and moral confusion. It's the social reality as it is, in which we are all in fact acrobats.
I don't really like shooting in the studio because of statics and limitations. It was a joke here of course. These chicks on girls' heads.

Life can be clear in some of its parts, but it's something quite mysterious as a whole, since every situation is a result of complicated collisions and tangles of our instincts and feelings with the environment and destiny beyond us. Sisters is a definition of 'inner relation' between various material and spiritual phenomena.

“You can deny, if you like, nearly all abstractions: justice, beauty, truth, goodness, mind, God. You can deny seriousness, but not play.” 
J. Huizinga

Play-art exists in the isolated world and is therefore similar to spiritual practices. Play-art involves not only creators, but also spectators, who abandon themselves to it, plunging into its universe, where they achieve harmony with themselves, or alternatively, complete differentiation. The existence of things esthetic is defined by our thinking. Trying to imagine the simplest things – a person, an animal, a family, nature – is never easy. Play-art has shifted from visual virtuosity to observation, meditations, deep contemplations, strong emotions and other tools of spiritual empiricism. Through ancient cultural practices, Oriental teachings and mythology, through zen gardens and essays of great philosophers, Bach’s anthems and Einstein’s Theory of General Relativity true artists of play-art get into the core mysteries of existence that are not accessible in any other way. Intentional desecration of values, the release of essential antinomies of culture, admittance of the relativity of priorities in art, polyphony of styles, dialogism of meanings. Many of these ideas can be found in the present cultural project. An artist and a photographer, Denmark and Russia, a woman and a man, youth and maturity create a sophisticated game of cultural paradigms and studies.

October 2017. Denmark, Copenhagen, Russisk Center for Videnskab og Kultur. Danish artist Mogens Andersen x Dasha Glukhova 
April 2017. Denmark, Aalboorg, ORDCRAFT
January/February 2016. Amsterdam, Home of Art gallery
April 2017. Denmark, Hjorring, Mogens Andersen Atelier
"Dasha Glukhova makes areal photography. It doesn’t mean she does photoshots in the air (although that happens, too). It’s just that all her pictures are full of air.
Dasha is the master of capturing the moment. But many photographers are. Unlike them, she’s capturing something so rapid you never know if it even lasted at all.

Although the approach is artistic, you can definitely spot the realism of the artist. When she shows the hidden glow of things, it’s non-retouched and unenhanced. The ephemeral reality, the transience of being, the light and the air. That’s some of the keywords of Dasha Glukhova’s photography you experience as a viewer". 

(c) Diana Windsdorm

Dasha Glukhova is a gallery photographer. 
She shoots stories in a documentary and poetic way. 
She works mostly on 35 mm film cameras, rarrelly on 120 mm film.  

Glukhova graduated from MSU university in art criticism department and advertising fild. She has defended a Phd about Creative technoly of PSA. For 5 years she investigated the difference between the creative process and the artist`s work in the context of the issues of the of the visual language based on different cultural codes.
After the period of theory study, Dasha entered MBA program of Amsterdam VU University.

Her works are in collections: Russisk Center for Videnskab og Kultur (Denmark), Mogens Andersen Atelier (Denmark) and others.

Selected clients:
Agent Provocateur, Vogue, Coca-Cola, Vanityfair, Samsung, Nike, Pepsi, Bloch, Time Out Magazine.

ARTIST STATEMENT 

Daria strives to maintain excitability, to look for beauty and romantic moments in everyday life. Nature or people in the frames have notes of nostalgia. Each frame seems to have its own special additional color, air and aura around, its own glow and instability. Which emphasizes the uniqueness. 
Daria photographed a lot of people at the beginning of her career. She has a personal photo project that she has been shooting for 8 years traveling around the world with her son, she takes pictures of him in the arms of flight attendants. During covid, Daria began to shoot fewer people, but more nature and air, trying to convey the smell of the moment. The latest works are dedicated to nature in its essence.

Strokke, Careva Contemporary, Riga, Latvia (2018). ORDCRAFT, Aalboorg, Denmark (2017). Continuum, Home of Art gallery, Amsterdam, Netherlands (2016).

Selected group exhibitions include

The body, Touchon&Co Gallery,  LA, USA (2019), Summer show, Rasmus gallery, Skagen,  Denmark (2017), Woman and nature, Mogens Andersen Atelier, Hjorring, Denmark (2017). The art of being together, Danish asrtist x Russian photographer, Russisk Center for Videnskab og Kultur, Copenhagen, Denmark (2017)
If you have a request for a consultation and my work on visual, you can find my work here  — https://duo-buro.com

Growing Wings / Life in Flight
This ongoing project has been at the center of my creative journey for over nine years, beginning with the birth of my son, a moment that profoundly shaped my life and perspective as an artist. I document children in motion — their gestures, exploration of nature, and instinctive freedom — capturing intimate, real-life moments that reveal growth, choice, and the possibilities of flight, both metaphorically and literally. It is a long-term investigation into presence, movement.
 
I ”This work is a long-term meditation on life, movement, and the invisible connections that shape our experience.

One of the most independent last kingdoms until 1975. Tibetan monks crossed the pass and settled there. Homeland of Tantras. The cleanest state between Nepal, Bhutan and Tibet. Ethnic villages where the spirit of Tibetan Buddhism and shamanistic rituals are mixed.

Changing, breathing. 
I needed this series of works about the simple and the valuable. 
It is pure and beautiful in itself, the basis of life. What we don't pay attention to. 
Pay attention to the textures of nature up close, as cleanly as possible and without distractions. 
Nettle, grass, drops, living in its essence.

One of the most closed city.
I feel to be so lucky to have an opportunity to be in and show you this authentic and lost-beauty.
